blockingIssues.description: The feature left `src/hooks/use-auto-lock.ts` importing and branching on `AUTO_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS` from `@/constants/auth`, so the PIN-era auth constants were not fully removed as claimed.

The reviewed navigator/get-initial-route changes route onboarded users straight to `Main` and ignore the normal locked biometric-ready path, so relaunch/locked sessions can bypass the biometric unlock gate.

The setup screen only matches raw `BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT` codes, but the real biometric vault maps those native errors to `VAULT_ERROR`, so real setup lockouts can fall through to the generic error path instead of the required lockout UX.

The unlock screen only handles raw lockout codes (plus a non-existent `VAULT_ERROR_BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT`), while the real vault surfaces lockouts as `VAULT_ERROR`, so actual OS lockouts render the generic error message instead of a clear lockout state.

The navigator only surfaces wallet-connect UI when `pending` exists. Deep links that fail while gated set wallet-connect `phase: 'error'` with `pending: null`, so once the user unlocks the app the queued failure is silently dropped instead of being surfaced.

Android screenshot protection is only a JS shim that silently no-ops unless an optional native `FlagSecure`-style module exists, and no such module is implemented or registered in this repo, so `FLAG_SECURE` is not actually enforced.

The restore success path mutates `hasCompletedOnboarding` and `hasIdentity` through `useSessionStore.setState(...)`, but those fields are only persisted via store helpers, so a kill/relaunch can rehydrate stale session flags and misroute a restored wallet.

The wallet-connect scanner permission flow returns early when `cameraRef.current` is null even though the `<Camera>` only renders after permission is granted, so the screen can remain stuck in the loading state while the tests bless the broken path.

The new Settings regression suite validates rows like `Lock wallet` and `Agent DID`, but the feature/contract called for About/version and external-link rows, so the required non-reset Settings surface is neither implemented nor verified.

The offline first-launch tests stub `@enbox/agent.initialize()` to immediate success, so they do not exercise the real initialization path under network failure and leave the offline onboarding assertion unproven.
The no-leakage full-flow test never executes `createIdentity()`, `unlockAgent()`, or `restoreFromMnemonic()`, so it does not actually scan all claimed lifecycle phases for mnemonic/secret logging.
The crash-reporter negative check only scans `package.json` and `src/`, not `ios/` and `android/`, so native-only crash reporter wiring would evade the asserted safeguard.

The `__DEV__` redaction test only validates a local `safeSerialize()` helper instead of any real devtools/logger serialization path, so it cannot prove that actual inspector snapshots redact the mnemonic.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten the skill's honesty gate so workers only report `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` after the transcript shows the required doc reads and, when applicable, a red-to-green test cycle.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: 11 of the 12 onboarding-ux reviews reported the same mismatch between the worker handoff and transcript skeleton.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Refresh the onboarding-ux auto-lock evidence pointers so the prescribed Jest command/path matches the current test suite.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `auto-lock-hook-teardown` review found the contract's prescribed evidence command had drifted away from the actual test file/name even though the feature implementation itself passed review.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document the current WalletConnect scanner permission/ref deadlock in shared state.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected because the scanner ref/permission problem is already a concrete product blocker that needs code fixes rather than a guidance-only update.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document an approved Android FLAG_SECURE implementation path in library guidance.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected because the immediate issue is missing native implementation; documenting the current placeholder shim would not resolve the blocking recovery-phrase security defect.

rejectedObservations.observation: Expand shared architecture docs to mention that reset must call `sessionStore.hydrate()` after clearing the wallet.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected because this is a narrow, non-blocking implementation detail that is better revisited after the reset flow and routing bugs are corrected.
